notes: This manifest seeds the minimum family baselines that the local salt_ndepend gate must materialize before product work can resume.
The first critical blocker family is the advanced color slider and direct-input contract because that regression escaped the earlier heuristic gate.
Repository-wide code quality stays an explicit packet input rather than an optional side report.
The current heuristic coverage audit is still required as an input even though it is not final authority by itself.
Native helper coverage remains mandatory for user-visible behavior changes.
Published-runtime proof cannot exist if the runtime is not republished cleanly first.
This is the headless published-runtime packet family for probes, sessions, descriptors, and generic sampling.
Artifact-producing runtime tools remain explicit packet inputs instead of hidden side tasks.
Viewer-backed runtime witnesses are mandatory packet inputs for user-visible behavior claims.
This is the first critical family because the color-pipeline slider regression escaped the earlier gate.
It must become the first family-parity packet before advanced-color work can resume.
The existing headless loaded-state scenario already proves that switching the Source lane function to root_proximity changes the published-runtime frame while preserving the loaded draft rows.
This behavior now needs its own explicit blocker packet instead of staying buried inside the generic advanced-color aggregate.
The existing headless loaded-state scenario already proves that adding a second Shape row changes the published-runtime frame while persisting the new row stack.
This behavior now needs its own explicit blocker packet instead of remaining folded into the generic advanced-color aggregate.
The existing loaded-state override regression already proves that invalid advanced-color drafts resynchronize away from disallowed root_proximity state.
This behavior now needs its own explicit blocker packet instead of hiding behind the slider-only contract.
